name: The Weeping Depths
subtitle: The sea remembers everything it swallows
theme: Perseverance / Hidden truths / The cost of power

shard.name: Seal Fragment of Water
shard.description: A deep blue crystal that weeps light continuously. It hums with the memory of countless tides.

scenes.narration: The Oracle's third marker leads to the coast of Aethoria's sunken west — a region that drowned when Valdris first shattered the Water Seal.
Nilou stands at the shoreline, watching the water with a strange expression — recognition, almost.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: In Sumeru, the sacred pools respond to dance. This water feels different. Grief-soaked.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Grief-soaked water. That is poetic. I might use that in a poem, if you do not mind.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: It is yours. Though I hope you give it a more cheerful ending than the sea did.

scenes.narration: Xiao crouches at the edge, scanning beneath the surface. Shadow-forms move in the depths — slow, circling.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: The Kraken. Ancient. Pre-dates Valdris. He corrupted it when he broke the Seal — turned a guardian into a warden.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: A creature once protector of these waters, now their jailer. There is a particular sadness in that.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: A sad fate. But also very large tentacles. Let us have a plan.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The descent into the Drowned Citadel takes them through corridors held up by nothing but old magic and shadow-power.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
pre_dialogue.text: Shadow creatures ahead. They are between us and the Fragment.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
pre_dialogue.text: Then we go through them. Together.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: Nilou — those water strikes hit harder than they look. Deceptively powerful for someone who mostly dances.

post_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
post_dialogue.text: Dancing requires great strength and precision. It just looks like something else.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: The Kraken is close. I can feel its karmic weight. Be ready.

scenes.narration: In the deepest chamber of the Drowned Citadel, the Sea Kraken awaits — a creature of immense age and corrupted sorrow, the Seal Fragment fused to its ancient body.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: It does not want to fight. The shadow compels it. Remember that.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: We will make it quick, then. It deserves that much.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: And we will free it from the corruption. Whatever it costs.

scenes.narration: As they ascend from the Drowned Citadel, Nilou pauses at the water's surface — now clearing as the shadow corruption retreats. She looks down at her reflection.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: The dancer who speaks to water.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: ...You are in the water?

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: The water carries everything that has ever been drowned in it. Memory. Grief. The weight of every world I have taken. It is honest, in its way. More honest than the living.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: What do you want from me?

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: You are a creature of beauty in a world that constantly destroys beautiful things. You felt this water's grief the moment you touched it. Does it not tire you — all that feeling, all that care — when the ending is always the same?

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: The ending being the same is exactly why the moments before it matter. You had that understanding once. The Oracle told us. You chose to discard it.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: I chose to survive it. There is a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: No. There is not. Not when survival costs everything else.

dialogue.speaker: narrator
dialogue.text: Valdris: ...Interesting.

scenes.narration: The reflection stills. The others have gathered close — Xiao with his hand near his weapon, Hutao watching the water surface, Ayaka standing beside Nilou.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Are you all right?

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: He tried to make me feel hopeless. He almost had an argument.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Almost?

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: I have danced in front of thousands of people and felt every single one of them. That is not a weakness. That is why I am still here.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: Ancient things are persuasive. They have had long enough to find the exact words that fit each person.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Then we keep moving. Together. That is what he cannot replicate — and that is exactly what will undo him.

scenes.narration: At the edge of the sunken coast, Rydia approaches the group with Lenneth at her side. The summoner's eyes glow softly with eidolon power, and the Valkyrie's presence commands respect.

dialogue.speaker: Rydia
dialogue.text: The others told us your burden. Four Fragments freed, and one final darkness stands before you. We did not journey here by chance.

dialogue.speaker: Lenneth
dialogue.text: I have judged countless souls across ages. This darkness — Valdris — he rejects judgment itself. That is a heresy I cannot ignore.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: You both carry power that echoes across worlds. What you have endured, what you have lost — it matters to this fight.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: The Oracle spoke of resonances. Perhaps we were always meant to find each other. Not by fate, but by recognition of the same truth.

scenes.narration: Rydia walks to the water's edge and closes her eyes. Her eidolons stir in the depths — shapes moving through shadow, ancient and patient.

dialogue.speaker: Rydia
dialogue.text: In my world, summoners commanded spirits as servants. Here, I understand them as allies. Partners. The Feymarch teaches patience. Everything in time.

dialogue.speaker: Nilou
dialogue.text: How many eidolons answer to you?

dialogue.speaker: Rydia
dialogue.text: Enough. Bahamut, Syldra, and others older still. They sense what I sense — that the cage Valdris built can be broken if we are willing to strike together.

scenes.narration: Hutao and Xiao sit with Lenneth and Rydia on the ruined stone of what was once a harbor.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: Lenneth. Valkyrie. That means you judge the souls of the dead, yes? Does that ever become... heavy?

dialogue.speaker: Lenneth
dialogue.text: I have carried the weight of a thousand judgments. And I will carry a thousand more. That is what being eternal means — the accumulation of every choice you ever made.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: Two thousand years of karma. I know that weight.

dialogue.speaker: Lenneth
dialogue.text: Then you understand. We who live beyond the span of mortals do not merely suffer loss — we multiply it across centuries. Yet here you stand. Still fighting.

scenes.narration: Rydia listens, her expression thoughtful, and speaks softly.

dialogue.speaker: Rydia
dialogue.text: In my world, I was young. I had only just begun to understand the eidolons. Now, pulled into this reality, I wonder if I ever truly had time to choose my own path.

dialogue.speaker: Hutao
dialogue.text: You are choosing it now. That is all any of us have.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: The past shapes us. But it does not decide what we do next. That is still ours.

dialogue.speaker: Rydia
dialogue.text: Then I choose this. I choose all of you. I choose to break the chains Valdris has woven.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The Kraken rises from the depths. Ancient. Immense. Its corruption pulses with the captured Seal Fragment, and it comes for them with blind fury.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: It does not want this fight. But it has no choice. That makes it more dangerous.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
pre_dialogue.text: Then we end this quickly. For its sake.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
pre_dialogue.text: For its sake, and for ours. Let us do what we came here to do.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The Kraken falls. As its shadow-form dissolves, the water Seal Fragment rises from the depths — clear and cold and weeping light.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: It is gone now. The karma is lifting. It can finally rest.

post_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
post_dialogue.text: I am glad it is free. I just wish we could have done it without the fighting.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: Sometimes the only kindness left is the one that ends suffering. Not comfortable — but true.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: ...I have seen enough to know she is right. Three Fragments down. One more before we truly face him.

scenes.narration: As the water settles, a figure descends from above — a dragoon knight, his armor marked with the sigil of Baron. His lance glows with aerial energy as he lands beside them.

dialogue.speaker: Kain
dialogue.text: I am Kain, Dragoon of Baron. I felt the Fragment's freedom from above — the sky itself sang with it. A barrier I did not know existed has lifted.

dialogue.speaker: Rydia
dialogue.text: Kain? From Baron? That kingdom fell ages ago in my world. How is it you stand here?

dialogue.speaker: Kain
dialogue.text: I do not know. But I was bound by a curse I could not break. Now, feeling that curse shatter — I followed the sensation here. To help.

dialogue.speaker: Xiao
dialogue.text: A warrior chained by his own oaths, freed by the breaking of chains elsewhere. I understand that more than most.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: Then let us bind ourselves together — not by curse, but by choice. Will you stand with us?

dialogue.speaker: Kain
dialogue.text: I will. My lance answers to a new oath now: the oath to see darkness broken.
